old_claim.note: Horse Collar provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Nailed Horseshoes and Horse Collar; Nailed horseshoes are hoof-protection hardware and do not require padded draft collars.

source_shape.source_locator: Study describes 103 medieval horseshoes from the Portable Antiquities Scheme dating c. 900-1600 CE, with Type 1 horseshoes dated c. 900-1100.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Nailed horseshoes are hoof-protection hardware and do not require padded draft collars.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Nailed Horseshoes chronology or adjacent technology without requiring Horse Collar as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Nailed Horseshoes treated Horse Collar as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Nailed Horseshoes no longer directly depends on Horse Collar; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: nailed_horseshoes must not directly depend on horse_collar unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows nailed_horseshoes is intentionally scoped only to cases that require horse_collar.
The graph splits nailed_horseshoes and reserves this id for a narrower technology directly dependent on horse_collar.
A stronger source demonstrates that horse_collar is chronologically and causally necessary for nailed_horseshoes.
short_reason: Nailed horseshoes are hoof-protection hardware and do not require padded draft collars.
